meta.title: Dr. Jorge Arce — Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery · v4 ClearPath
meta.description: Board-certified oral & maxillofacial surgeon. Twenty-seven years between Colombia and California on the reconstructions other surgeons refer out.

sections.title: A path back to eating, smiling, living.
sections.text: Dr. Jorge Arce is a board-certified oral & maxillofacial surgeon. For twenty-seven years he has walked the difficult path with his patients — the complex extractions, reconstructions, and full-arch implant cases other surgeons refer out — with confidence and care.

sections.title: From Colombia to the South Bay — a career spent on <e>the difficult cases</e>.
sections.text: Trained in Colombia, built in California. Board certified in implantology, guided by one conviction: difficult work, done with care, gives people their lives back.

steps.text: Start with a long, unhurried first consultation. Your history, your scans, your questions — and an honest read of what is possible. No pressure, just clarity. If the right answer is not to operate, you will hear that plainly.

Every case is mapped before anyone operates. Twenty-seven years on complex extractions, reconstruction, and full-arch cases taught Dr. Arce one unglamorous principle: take the time the case actually needs.
Surgery with steady hands, then restoration — function first, then structure, then confidence. The smile is the last step, not the first promise.
Long-term maintenance that protects the work — and a full report back to your referring dentist, every time. A restored smile is a relationship, not a transaction.

sections.title: The disciplines of nearly three decades.
sections.text: Not a list of services — the work refined across twenty-seven years of practice.

cards.d: From single implants to full-arch All-On-X reconstruction, planned and placed with precision.
Rebuilding function and structure where bone and tissue have been lost.
The difficult extractions and surgeries other clinicians prefer to refer elsewhere.
Long-term care that protects the work of a restored smile.
Identifying and addressing conditions affecting teeth, gums, and bone.
Clarity and direction for patients facing complex decisions.

sections.statement: From the first consultation to the final restoration, these numbers reflect a career spent <s>walking the path together.</s>
sections.side: Not milestones for their own sake — each one stands for patients who were told it couldn't be done, and the work, dedication, and care behind every step.

sections.title: The people who walked it.
sections.text: Patients and referring doctors, on twenty-seven years of quiet precision.

cards.q: I had been told by two surgeons it was impossible. Dr. Arce took the time no one else did. I can eat, smile, and speak again.
When a case is beyond what I can handle, Jorge is the first call I make. My patients always come back grateful — and so do I.
The most thorough and honest surgeon I have ever met. He treated my father like family.
Calm, precise, and genuinely kind. He explained every step until I understood. The result speaks for itself.
I refer my most complex cases to Dr. Arce without hesitation. Twenty-seven years shows.
I was terrified of the procedure. He never rushed me. Best decision I have made for my health.
Reliable, communicative, and surgically excellent. He sends my patients back with a full report every time.
Years of pain, gone. I only wish I had found him sooner. I recommend Dr. Arce to everyone I know.
The level of care and follow-up is rare. My patients trust him, and that trust reflects back on my practice.

sections.title:
Your questions.
Answered.
sections.text: Not sure what to expect? These answers might help you feel more confident as you begin.

items.q: What happens at a first consultation?
items.a: A long, unhurried conversation. Dr. Arce reviews your history and imaging, examines the site, and gives you an honest read of what is possible — including, when it is the right answer, the recommendation not to operate.

items.q: I was told my case is impossible. Is it?
items.a: Sometimes it is — and you deserve to hear that plainly. But many of the cases in this practice arrived with that exact sentence. Complex cases are not a detour from this work; they are the work.

items.q: How do I know if I'm a candidate for implants?
items.a: Bone volume, gum health, medical history, and timing all matter. The free patient guide below covers the signs — and a consultation settles it definitively with imaging.

items.q: Do you work with referring dentists?
items.a: Constantly — much of this practice arrives by referral. Every referred patient goes back with a full surgical report, and the restorative work stays with your general dentist.

sections.text: Beyond the operating room, Dr. Arce lectures and mentors — training the next generation of surgeons and speaking on implantology and complex reconstruction.
